Logistics Topic > International Settlement > Guide to SWIFT Codes for CIB Egypt

Guide to SWIFT Codes for CIB Egypt

Published: 09/03/2025
SWIFT Code Egypt Remittance CIB Bank
This article provides a detailed overview of the SWIFT code for Commercial International Bank (CIB) Egypt, including the head office code CIBEEGCXXXX and instructions on how to find branch-specific codes. It also offers important considerations for international money transfers and receipts, helping readers avoid errors and successfully complete cross-border transactions. The information aims to facilitate smooth and efficient remittance processes involving CIB Egypt.

Imagine working hard to save money for your loved ones in Egypt, only to have your international transfer disappear due to an incorrect SWIFT code. This frustrating scenario can be easily avoided with the right information about Commercial International Bank (CIB) Egypt's banking codes.

Understanding SWIFT Codes and Their Importance

The SWIFT (Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication) code, also known as BIC (Bank Identifier Code), serves as a bank's international identification number. This unique 8-11 character code contains essential information including bank code, country code, location code, and optional branch code.

Using an incorrect SWIFT code may result in failed transactions, delayed payments, or returned funds with associated fees. Verifying the accurate code before initiating international transfers is crucial for successful transactions.

Commercial International Bank (Egypt) SWIFT Code: CIBEEGCXXXX

As Egypt's largest private bank with an extensive branch network, Commercial International Bank (CIB) uses the primary SWIFT code CIBEEGCXXXX for international transfers to its Egyptian accounts.

Breaking down the code's components:

  • CIBE : Bank code for Commercial International Bank (Egypt)
  • EG : Country code for Egypt
  • CX : Location code for Cairo
  • XXX : Default branch code for head office

Important Note: Branch-Specific SWIFT Codes May Apply

While CIBEEGCXXXX serves as CIB's primary SWIFT code, individual branches may use different codes. For transfers to specific branches, recipients should verify their exact SWIFT code through bank statements, online banking, or direct bank contact.

Locating CIB Branch SWIFT Codes

Several methods exist to find branch-specific SWIFT codes:

  • Contact the recipient: Request their branch's exact code from bank documents or customer service
  • Reach CIB directly: Use official bank channels including website, customer service hotlines, or email
  • SWIFT code directories: Online tools may provide codes, though verification with the bank is recommended

When to Use the Primary SWIFT Code (CIBEEGCXXXX)

The head office code is appropriate when:

  • Sending international transfers to CIB Egypt accounts without branch details
  • Recipients cannot provide specific branch codes
  • CIB processes payments centrally through its main office
  • Using a widely recognized default code is necessary

Critical Verification Before Transfer

Before initiating SWIFT transfers, carefully confirm:

  • Accurate SWIFT code matching the recipient bank
  • Correct account number for the beneficiary
  • Exact matching of recipient name with bank records

Even minor discrepancies may cause transaction failures. If errors occur, immediately contact your bank for resolution.

Receiving International Transfers to CIB Accounts

To receive overseas funds in CIB accounts, provide senders with:

  • Your full legal name as registered with the bank
  • Complete CIB account number
  • Correct SWIFT code (CIBEEGCXXXX or branch-specific code)
  • Bank address: 66, 68 Mohy Eldin Abu El, Cairo, Egypt

Conclusion

Understanding Commercial International Bank (Egypt)'s SWIFT code system is essential for successful international transactions. While CIBEEGCXXXX serves as the primary code, branch-specific variations may exist. Thorough verification of all transfer details ensures smooth financial transactions to Egypt.
